scores.correctness_notes: The agent partially covers events (certificate_available, certificate_expiring, certificate_invalidated match the gold standard's note) and some 'when to request' scenarios, but misses the interface contract focus (CSR requirements, error codes 101/102/103/201), and the gold standard explicitly notes that event names are part of the Python API not the interface spec — the agent treats them as primary.
scores.hallucination_notes: Extensive fabrication: TLSCertificatesRequiresV4 class, CertificateRequestAttributes, Mode.UNIT/Mode.APP, renewal_relative_time parameter (0.5–1.0 range, 90% default), refresh_events parameter, v4 internal Juju secret expiry renewal mechanism, get_certificate_requests()/get_outstanding_certificate_requests() methods, AllCertificatesInvalidatedEvent, CertificateCreationRequestEvent/CertificateRevocationRequestEvent for v3 provider side — none of these appear in the gold standard source and many are likely from training data about a different/older library version.
